Non-material anatomical entity of three dimensions, that is generated by morphogenetic or other physiologic processes; is surrounded by one or more anatomical structures; contains one or more organism substances or anatomical structures.
Ceratobranchial 5 tooth which is the posteriormost tooth in the ventral tooth row.
Portion of tissue that is the caudal gigantocellular portion of the magnocellular preoptic nucleus. From Neuroanatomy of the Zebrafish Brain.
Multi-tissue structure that forms the transparent front part of the eye, covering the iris, pupil and anterior chamber.

Ventromedial region of somite 16 that will form vertebral cartilages and connective tissues. The sclerotome lies ventral to the myotome. Cells of the sclerotome will migrate dorsally to surround the notochord and neural tube.
Intercellular space surrounded by the basal surface of the hepatocytes that collect bile.
The anterior portion of the adenohypophysis. Comprised of the proximal and rostral pars anterior.

Bony process that develops as a dorsally  projecting cartilaginous extension from the basidorsal.  The ascending process is longer relative to the manubrium in larvae (5.1-11.0 mm). By 13.0 mm, the ascending process appears highly reduced in size.

A mature osteoblast that has become embedded in the bone matrix. They occupy a small cavity, called lacuna, in the matrix and are connected to adjacent osteocytes via protoplasmic projections called canaliculi.
A cell of the intermediate pituitary that produces melanocyte stimulating hormone.

Artery that extends horizontally across the brain midline which is fed by the caudal division of the internal carotid artery. Three pairs of central arteries extend from the basal communicating artery (BCA), the anterior, middle and  posterior mesencephalic central arteries. The BCA does not correspond to the anterior communicating artery of the mammalian circle of Willis.  The BCA is unpaired.
Simple squamous epithelium that lines the capsule of the pronephric glomerulus.

A supporting cell for the developing female gamete in the ovary of mammals. They develop from the coelomic epithelial cells of the gonadal ridge. Granulosa cells form a single layer around the mammalian oocyte in the primordial ovarian follicle and advance to form a multilayered cumulus oophorus surrounding the ovum in the Graafian follicle. The major functions of granulosa cells include the production of steroids and LH receptors.
A zone of delayed differentiation at the midbrain hindbrain boundary.  The IZ separates midbrain from anterior hindbrain neuronal clusters and has been described in all vertebrates.

After the primitive prosencephalic artery forms it becomes disconnected from the  cranial division of the internal carotid artery and instead it connects to the anterior mesencephalic central artery and the communicating vessels of the palatocerebral artery.
Space between the renal glomerulus and the glomerular capsule.  Renal filtrate collects here then passes into the renal tubule.
Epibranchial placode that gives rise to vagal ganglion 3.
Retinal cone cell with a long outer segment.  The long single cone cells are sensitive to blue light and are flanked by the long double cone cells.
Portion of organism substance that is a clear fluid that occupies the space in synovial joints.

Is a mandibular muscle that consists of three subdivisions A1, A2, and A3. All three originate on the hyomandibula and suspensorium but each has a distinct insertion. A1 inserts on the maxilla, A2 inserts along the caudal margin of the articular and A3 inserts on the medial surface of the articular.
A progenitor cell restricted to the myeloid lineage.
A muscle cell that is small, darkly colored, with high lipid and mitochondrial content. Slow muscle cells are located superficial to the fast muscle cell in the myotome of somite 17.
Portion of presumptive mesoderm fated to become ventral mesoderm. Determined by fate mapping.
Replacement bone that forms within cartilage.

Bilaterally paired neurocranium cartilage which is initially plate-like and formed from the anterior fusion between the bilaterally paired trabecula cranii. It grows dorsally between the laminae orbitonasales to attain a complex shape.
Hindbrain interneuron that is part of rhombomere 3 with one lateral neuron in each hemi-rhombomere.
Thin layer of loose connective tissue which lies beneath the intestinal  epithelium. The intestinal lamina propria is less complex then the corresponding mammalian tissue.

A secretory cell found in pancreatic acini that secretes digestive enzymes and mucins. This cell is a typical zymogenic cell, has a basal nucleus and basophilic cytoplasm consisting of regular arrays of granular endoplasmic reticulum with mitochondria and dense secretory granules.
Diencephalic nucleus that is part of the synencephalon and is located adjacent to the caudal commissure. From Neuroanatomy of the Zebrafish Brain.

Extraocular muscle innervated by the trochlear nerve.  The dorsal rectus and dorsal oblique muscles have overlapping insertions at the scleral-corneal junction on the dorsal side of the adult eye, though they have distinct insertion sites in 5dpf fish.   The dorsal oblique originates separately from the rectus muscles. The unsegmented paraxial head mesoderm contributes to the dorsal oblique muscles.   All the oblique extraocular muscles originate together from the anterior ethmoid plate.
Portion of the renal tubule which is just posterior to the proximal convoluted tubule.
The hypaxial region of somite 14 derived body wall muscle. The hypaxial region is ventral to the horizontal myoseptum.
A cell capable of processing and presenting lipid and protein antigens to T cells in order to initiate an immune response.
Muscle system of the pharyngeal region.
Portion of the dermis characterized by a well-arranged, plywood-like, collagenous network.

A terminally differentiated, post-mitotic, antibody secreting cell of the B cell lineage with the phenotype CD138-positive, surface immunonoglobulin-negative, and MHC Class II-negative. Plasma cells are oval or round with extensive rough endoplasmic reticulum, a well-developed Golgi apparatus, and a round nucleus having a characteristic cartwheel heterochromatin pattern and are devoted to producing large amounts of immunoglobulin.
Composed of 7-10 layers of helically arranged smooth muscle cells surrounded by a fine network of collagen, reticular, and elastic fibrils.
Diencephalic nucleus which is part of the dorsal hypothalamic zone and is located periventricularly. From Neuroanatomy of the Zebrafish Brain.
Organism subdivision which is the part of the body which consists of the cranial and pharyngeal regions.
Rib that articulates with the parapophysis of the sixth centrum.
Swellings of tissue present between the endocardial and myocardial cell layers that will give rise to the interstitial cells of the cardiac valves.
Dermal bone lying below the opercle. The subopercle is a paired bone.
Granule cell that is part of the olfactory bulb.
Portion of somite 18 that gives rise to body wall muscle masses.

Muscle of the dorsal fin that are thin, arise from the lateral edge of fin bases, and attach to the skin just above the epaxial muscle.  The inclinator muscles bend the fin sideways.  There is one dorsal inclinator for each dorsal fin lepidotrichium.
The layer within the retina where the photoreceptor cell receptor segments reside. This layer is closest to the sclera.
Outflow artery from the heart to the aortic arches.
Portion of tissue on the side of the lumen of the neural tube.
A cell of the inner of the three germ layers of the embryo.

Hindbrain nucleus that is located in ventral rhombomeres 1-3 and gives rise to ascending fibers innervating most areas of the brain.  Early in development, both the superior raphe nucleus emits rostrally and caudally directed projections. However, in the adult, the long projections of superior raphe nucleus  restricted to anterior.
A ring of conduction tissue that forms in the atrioventricular canal.
CNS white matter, axonal tract between the preoptic region and ventral diencephalon.
Telencephalic white matter which extends between the olfactory bulb and the ipsilateral ventral telencephalon and carries secondary olfactory fibers. From Neuroanatomy of the Zebrafish Brain.
Sensory trigeminal nucleus located in the most rostral position.  It lies immediately caudal to the secondary gustatory nucleus.

Portion of tissue that gives rise to the lens. The lens of the zebrafish forms by delamination of lens placodal cells and not through invagination. This results in a solid spherical mass as opposed to a hollow lens vesicle. Detachment of the solid lens vesicle of zebrafish at 24-26 hpf is accomplished in part by apoptosis, similar to mammals.
Tooth row that is the ventralmost row of teeth on ceratobranchial 5 bone.
Parasympathetic neurons are part of the parasympathetic nervous system and the cell bodies lie in the brain and sacral region of the spinal cord. The neurons are mainly cholinergic.
Stripe of tissue on the dorsal side of the fish, fated to become the dorsal region of the median fin fold.
An endocrine cell that is located in the epithelium of the gastrointestinal tract.
A muscle cell that is small, darkly colored, with high lipid and mitochondrial content. Slow muscle cells are located superficial to the fast muscle cell in the myotome of somite 21.
A non-striated muscle that is composed of spindle-shaped cells.  Smooth muscle usually is organized into sheets that line cavitated organs.
Diencephalic tract which is located in the vicinity of the dorsal diencephalon and mesencephalon and connects the pretectal nuclei. From Neuroanatomy of the Zebrafish Brain.
Compound organ that does not contain macroscopic anatomical spaces.
An anatomical structure that is an opening or tube that connects two distinct anatomical spaces.
Portion of neural tube that gives rise to the midbrain hindbrain boundary.
Central region of the glomerular layer of the olfactory bulb.
Octaval nerve motor nucleus that is initially located within rhombomere 6.
The inner of the two layers of the blastoderm that forms during gastrulation and give rise to the definitive mesoderm and endoderm.
System responsible for generating hematopoietic cells.
Embryonic structure that forms from the anterior gut epithelium at the level of the second somite that then matures to form the swim bladder.
Fourth lepidotrichium of pelvic fin.
Embryonic structure that is the dome of cytoplasm that segregates from the yolk toward the animal pole during and after the one cell-stage, and which undergoes cleavage.
Vein that drains the median palatocerebral vein into the optic vein.
Small rounded to egg-shaped cartilage bone that sits in an anteriolateral depression on the prevomer. Articulates with the autopalatine in all cypriniforms.
Portion of somite 5 that gives rise to body wall muscle masses.
Multi-tissue structure that is the dorsal part of the midbrain and develops from the alar plate and is the most complex layered structure in the zebrafish brain.
Bone that forms as a replacement of another structural tissue.
Anatomical cluster that is the series of dermal bones associated with the infraorbital sensory canal located under and posterior to the eye.
